title: Train, validation, test and leakage deterministic AI draft bank

publication.changeSummary: Misconception MCQ, evidence-boundary fill-gap, and deterministic offline Python lab for Train, validation, test and leakage; all items remain blocked from formal exams pending independent human review.

items.title: Train, validation, test and leakage: reject the overclaim
items.prompt: Which statement is source-bounded and corrects this AI misconception? Any random 80/20 split prevents leakage as long as the target column is removed from model inputs.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Choose the statement that names mechanism, evidence conditions, and the boundary of the claim rather than a fluent or benchmark-based overgeneralization.

feedback.correct: Correct. Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the overclaim. Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.

criteria.description: Selects the source-bounded correction and rejects the documented AI overclaim.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against cited sources and ensure the distractor does not normalize unsafe or overstated claims.

options.text: Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.
Any random 80/20 split prevents leakage as long as the target column is removed from model inputs.

answer.explanation: Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.

items.title: Train, validation, test and leakage: canonical evidence boundary
items.prompt: Complete the exact source-bounded correction phrase; the complete sentence must remain an honest beginner-to-intermediate claim.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Retrieve the short checkpoint from the correction and ensure it narrows rather than expands the evidence claim.

feedback.correct: Correct. The checkpoint is 'deployment unit'. Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.
feedback.incorrect: The phrase must preserve the technical and responsible-AI boundary. Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.

criteria.description: Supplies the exact canonical phrase that completes the bounded source-aligned correction.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the accepted phrase and technically equivalent variants before learner use.
items.template: Split by the [[checkpoint]] and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.

answer.explanation: Split by the deployment unit and time structure before fitting preprocessing or tuning, and reserve the test set for the frozen final procedure.

items.title: Train, validation, test and leakage: deterministic offline Python lab
items.prompt: Read entity,split rows and print sorted entity ids appearing in more than one split, or clean.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted unreviewed draft; independent human technical, source, pedagogy, safety, accessibility, and answer-oracle review is required before formal-exam eligibility.

hints: Implement only the stated offline contract, run the visible boundary, and do not infer a real model, safety, fairness, or deployment claim from this toy result.

feedback.correct: The deterministic offline reference checks match the bounded contract; this does not establish real-world AI quality or safety.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check differs; inspect parsing, algorithm assumptions, boundary behavior, and exact output before retrying.

criteria.description: Passes all visible and hidden deterministic offline tests while preserving the stated evidence and non-claim boundary.

privateAuthorNotes: Offline stdlib-only task. Human reviewer must verify algorithm, fixtures, overclaim boundary, and educational validity before any exam use.

answer.explanation: Grouping by deployment entity exposes row-level leakage across train, validation, and test partitions.
